title: Knowledge
description: Validated Evidence, organized and interpreted within a commercial context. The system's current best understanding of a subject, derived exclusively from one or more validated Evidence objects. See kernel/ontology/knowledge.md for full canonical definition.

knowledge_id.description: Canonical identifier. Never reused, even across versions.

subject_ref.description: The entity this knowledge is about.

knowledge_type.description: Domain classification. Open enum extended via Specification layer. e.g. merchant_profile, delivery_pattern, payment_behavior, identity_verification, risk_indicator.

statement.description: The interpreted claim in normalized form. What the system understands to be true about the subject.

evidence_refs.description: One or more evidence_id values this knowledge is derived from. All referenced Evidence must have validation_status: validated.

derivation_method.description: How Evidence was turned into Knowledge.

confidence.description: Weighted aggregate of the backing Evidence confidence scores, adjusted for derivation method. Mutable/recalculable.

status.description: State machine: forming → active → stale → superseded | retracted.

supersedes.description: Prior knowledge_id this version replaces, if any.

retraction_reason.description: Required if status = retracted. Immutable once set.

graph_links.description: Append-only. Commercial Graph entities this knowledge informs.

created_at.description: When this Knowledge object was first derived.

last_evaluated_at.description: When confidence and validity were last recomputed.

archived.description: One-way transition false -> true. Knowledge is archived, never deleted.

entity_type.description: References a Kernel ontology entity, e.g. Merchant, Organization, Transaction.

valid_until.description: When this Knowledge expires. Null = indefinite until superseded or retracted.

decay_rate.description: How fast confidence degrades over time. Assigned per knowledge_type — e.g. identity_verification decays slowly, delivery_pattern decays fast.

staleness_threshold.description: Confidence floor below which status automatically transitions to stale.

derived_by.description: Engine or process that produced this Knowledge object.

evidence_snapshot.description: Immutable. Snapshot of evidence_id list and confidence scores at derivation time. Preserves auditability even after backing Evidence is updated.

derivation_chain.description: Append-only log of every re-evaluation or re-scoring event after initial derivation.

actor.description: System component or identity that triggered the event.

trigger.description: What caused this event, e.g. evidence_superseded, confidence_decay, manual_review.
